frontier_justification: This direction remains a May 2026 frontier because formal theorem proving has advanced rapidly on competition-style problems, yet research mathematics is not only proof search over a fixed statement. The unresolved frontier is bridging informal exploration, conjecture generation, definition selection, Lean-grounded proof, and human-understandable mathematical narratives.

papers.title: Generative Language Modeling for Automated Theorem Proving
papers.key_contribution: First application of large-scale transformer language models (GPT-f) to automated theorem proving in Metamath, producing proofs accepted into the main Metamath library.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Traditional automated theorem provers rely on hand-crafted heuristics and struggle with the combinatorial explosion of proof search in formal mathematics.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Train a GPT-style transformer on Metamath proof steps; use the model to generate tactic predictions one step at a time, guided by best-first search over the proof tree.
idea_genome.observation_genome: GPT-f discovers novel short proofs accepted into the Metamath library; first time a deep learning system contributes proofs adopted by a formal mathematics community.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Limited to Metamath's substitution-based proof system; no curriculum learning or expert iteration; proof search relies on a fixed model without online learning.

papers.title: Formal Mathematics Statement Curriculum Learning
papers.key_contribution: Expert iteration (proof search interleaved with learning) on a curriculum of formal statements dramatically outperforms static proof search, achieving state-of-the-art on miniF2F.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Static language models plateau on formal theorem proving because they cannot learn from their own proof search experience; harder problems require iterative self-improvement.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Expert iteration: alternate between proof search (generating successful proofs) and retraining the model on discovered proofs; apply to a manually curated curriculum of increasingly difficult statements in Lean.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Expert iteration at the same compute budget dramatically outperforms proof-search-only baselines; achieves SOTA on miniF2F benchmark; solves olympiad-level problems without ground-truth proofs.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Requires manually curated problem statements; expert iteration is computationally expensive; limited to Lean 3; no integration with informal mathematical reasoning.

papers.title: HyperTree Proof Search for Neural Theorem Proving
papers.key_contribution: AlphaZero-inspired online training procedure with HyperTree Proof Search (HTPS) achieves 82.6% on Metamath, dramatically outperforming GPT-f's 56.5%.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Expert iteration uses offline retraining cycles; proof search lacks the principled exploration-exploitation trade-off of game-playing systems like AlphaZero.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: HyperTree Proof Search: MCTS-style search over proof trees with a learned value function and policy network; online training interleaves search and model updates continuously; proof state represented as a hypergraph.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Online HTPS training reaches 82.6% on Metamath held-out theorems (vs 56.5% for GPT-f); learned value function provides reliable node scoring for search.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Trained and evaluated primarily on Metamath; online training is extremely compute-intensive; does not leverage informal mathematical reasoning or autoformalization.

papers.title: DeepSeek-Prover: Advancing Theorem Proving in LLMs through Large-Scale Synthetic Data
papers.key_contribution: Generates large-scale synthetic Lean 4 theorem-proof pairs via autoformalization and compiler filtering, enabling LLM-based provers to achieve strong results without human-annotated proofs.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Human-annotated formal proofs are scarce; existing provers require expensive expert iteration; the gap between informal math corpora and formal proof libraries limits training data.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Use LLMs to autoformalize natural language math into Lean 4 statements; generate candidate proofs; filter with the Lean compiler for correctness; train DeepSeek-Prover on the resulting large-scale synthetic dataset.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Large-scale synthetic data generation produces millions of verified theorem-proof pairs; DeepSeek-Prover achieves competitive results on miniF2F without expert iteration.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Autoformalization introduces errors that compiler filtering cannot fully catch; single-pass proof generation without search; no reinforcement learning or value-guided exploration.

papers.title: Olympiad-level formal mathematical reasoning with reinforcement learning
papers.key_contribution: AlphaProof achieves silver-medal performance at the 2024 IMO by combining AlphaZero-style RL with autoformalized training on 100 million formal problems and test-time RL for deep problem-specific adaptation.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Prior neural theorem provers solve competition problems individually but cannot match human olympiad performance across a full problem set; test-time adaptation to novel hard problems is lacking.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: AlphaZero-inspired RL agent trained on 100M autoformalized problems in Lean; test-time RL generates and learns from millions of problem variants at inference time for deep adaptation to specific competition problems.
idea_genome.observation_genome: AlphaProof earns 28/42 points at IMO 2024 (silver medal level); solves the hardest problem (P6) which only 5 human contestants solved; 100% formally verified proofs.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Requires enormous compute for test-time RL (days per problem); cannot produce human-readable informal proofs; limited to problems that can be autoformalized; geometry solved by separate AlphaGeometry system.

papers.title: Aristotle: IMO-level Automated Theorem Proving
papers.key_contribution: Achieves gold-medal performance at IMO 2025 by solving 5 of 6 problems with formally verified Lean 4 proofs, integrating informal natural-language planning with formal proof search via MCTS.

idea_genome.problem_genome: AlphaProof's test-time RL is extremely expensive and cannot bridge informal mathematical intuition with formal proofs; a unified system that plans informally and proves formally is needed.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: MCTS-based proof search in Lean 4 with a learned value function in the spirit of Expert Iteration and AlphaZero; integrates informal natural-language reasoning for high-level proof planning with formal tactic generation for rigorous verification.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Formally verified solutions for 5 of 6 IMO 2025 problems (gold-medal equivalent); surpasses AlphaProof's silver-medal performance; demonstrates that integrating informal planning with formal verification yields stronger results than pure RL-based proving.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Still requires massive compute for search; informal-to-formal bridge is fragile on problems outside competition math; cannot handle open-ended conjecture generation; limited to problems expressible in Lean 4.

edges.evolution_focus: Expert iteration adds an online self-improvement loop to GPT-f's static language model approach. The core mechanism (transformer generating tactic steps) is preserved, but the model now iteratively retrains on its own discovered proofs, creating a curriculum learning dynamic that dramatically improves performance without changing the underlying architecture.

HTPS hybridizes the expert iteration paradigm from formal theorem proving with AlphaZero's MCTS framework from game playing. The proof search tree is reconceived as a game tree with learned value functions, merging two distinct research lineages (LLM-based proving and RL-based game playing) into a unified online training procedure.

DeepSeek-Prover radiates the neural theorem proving paradigm into the LLM-scale regime by replacing expensive online training with large-scale synthetic data generation. Rather than the compute-intensive MCTS search of HTPS, it leverages the autoformalization capabilities of frontier LLMs to generate millions of training pairs, adapting the approach to a data-scaling paradigm suited to modern LLM infrastructure.

AlphaProof fuses DeepSeek-Prover's autoformalization-at-scale approach (100M problems) with HTPS's AlphaZero-style RL training, and adds a novel test-time RL mechanism. This hybridization of massive synthetic data, RL-guided proof search, and inference-time adaptation produces the first system to reach olympiad medal level in formal mathematics.

Aristotle represents a speciation event: while AlphaProof relies on purely formal RL search, Aristotle introduces a fundamentally new informal-formal integration where natural language planning guides formal proof search. This creates a distinct species of theorem prover that bridges human mathematical intuition with machine verification, achieving gold-medal performance through a qualitatively different architecture.

open_question: Aristotle-style IMO provers show strong formal proof search on well-posed Lean problems, but research mathematics requires formulating conjectures, choosing definitions, and deciding which lemmas matter before a formal target exists. What system would connect informal exploration, Lean-grounded proof search, and conjecture generation for open-ended mathematical research?

question.name: Question
question.description: Present only the domain/subfield and the curated May-2026 research question; ask the model to propose a next-step research direction in Formal Mathematics / Automated Theorem Proving.

library.name: Library
library.description: Present the curated research frontier plus 6 trace papers in random order with paper titles and brief abstracts, but without explicit lineage order, genome fields, or dynamics labels; ask the model to propose the next research direction.

lineage.name: Lineage
lineage.description: Present the curated research frontier plus the ordered trace with genome metadata, GenomeDiff-style change evidence, and parent-child relationships; ask the model to predict the next evolutionary step without naming predefined category labels.

frontier_evidence.title: Aristotle: IMO-level Automated Theorem Proving
frontier_evidence.unresolved_gap: Formal theorem provers can solve precisely stated problems, but open-ended research mathematics still requires conjecture formulation, definition choice, informal exploration, and proof planning before formal targets exist.
frontier_evidence.why_frontier: Used as the May 2026 frontier anchor: the arena question targets a still-open limitation at the endpoint of this lineage, not a solved benchmark task.

frontier_status.manual_review: Re-audited on 2026-05-05 for appendix release: active arena trace has a 2025/2026 frontier endpoint, a non-solved open research question, valid forward lineage edges, and generation settings aligned with post-hoc dynamics evaluation.
frontier_status.prompt_role: The same research question is shown in Question, Library, and Lineage settings; only the supporting literature/lineage context changes.
